slides.title: How area follows the radius
slides.body: Grow the circle. The new area is added as a thin rim. Its width is dr and its length is the circumference, so dA = 2πr · dr.

slides.title: Relate the rates
slides.body: Differentiate the measure with respect to its changing dimension, then multiply by the given rate.

problem.prompt: A sphere's radius grows at dr/dt = 3 cm/s. At r = 4 cm, how fast is its volume changing?
problem.hint: dV/dr = 4πr² = 4π·4². Multiply by dr/dt = 3. You can answer with π.

slides.title: Velocity and acceleration
slides.body: Play the motion. Velocity is the first derivative of position; acceleration is the second. Watch how each vector points.

slides.title: Acceleration at an instant
slides.body: The bug's position along the track is s(t). Find its acceleration at the given time.

feedback.wrong: a(t) = s″(t). Differentiate s(t) twice, then substitute t = 2.

slides.title: The Intermediate Value Theorem
slides.body: Place A and B on the curve, then enter any value between f(a) and f(b). A continuous curve must reach it. Reveal the point c where it does.

slides.title: Apply the Intermediate Value Theorem
slides.body: Points A and B sit on the curve. Use the given equation to answer the question.

feedback.wrong: The IVT only guarantees values that lie between f(a) and f(b).
